metric_value: Commercial packaged security scanner vs SATAN's free Unix-based approach; ready-to-install; no Unix expertise required
notes: Key differentiation: commercialized and packaged vs DIY free tools

metric_value: Covers all Unix platforms, Windows NT, AS/400, S/390, PC clients, routers, all firewalls, web servers, modems, printers, storage—any IP-connected device
notes: Comprehensive IP-based coverage is ISS core value proposition

metric_value: NCSA uses ISS Firewall Scanner as core part of its firewall accreditation program certifying independently-developed firewall products
source_page: Known By the Company One Keeps
notes: Third-party institutional endorsement; NCSA certification program legitimizes ISS technology

metric_value: NCSA certification programs led insurance carriers to underwrite liability coverage for NCSA-certified web sites
source_page: Known By the Company One Keeps
notes: Very early documentation of cyber insurance emerging in 1996; now a major market

metric_value: More than 130 distributors worldwide; partnerships with Ernst & Young, Price Waterhouse, Raptor, Trusted Information Systems
notes: Significant channel reach for a young company in 1996

metric_value: Operates on Windows NT, HP-UX, IBM AIX, SunOS, Solaris, Intel Linux
notes: Multi-platform support for the scanning console itself

metric_value: NT security policies are not enforced across the network; Microsoft removed security controls available in NT 3.50 from 3.51 and 4.0
notes: Critical security finding about NT; security controls regressed in new versions

metric_value: For experienced IS managers, networked application security appears like an egg—hard on the outside and soft on the inside; correctly configured externally but internally vulnerable
source_page: Network Computing Security — A Fragile Egg!
notes: Aberdeen metaphor for perimeter-only security; precursor to Zero Trust security model

metric_value: Incorrectly configured routers, web servers, applications, operating environments, modems on internal sub-networks can shatter protective safeguards without warning
source_page: Network Computing Security — A Fragile Egg!
notes: Internal misconfiguration as attack vector; prescient of modern insider threat / misconfiguration breach patterns

metric_value: ISS's technology appears to be leading the industry; continuously developing new capabilities; security is ongoing arms race
notes: Aberdeen concludes ISS is technology leader in network security assessment

metric_value: IBM acquired ISS for $1.93B in October 2006, confirming technology leadership and strategic value; ISS integrated into IBM Security Systems Division
notes: $1.93B acquisition premium validates Aberdeen's technology leadership assessment

metric_name: Network Security Assessment as Enterprise Requirement
metric_value: Aberdeen recommends IS organizations building web-based e-commerce applications evaluate SAFEsuite as foundation for comprehensive security program
notes: Predicts network security assessment becomes mandatory for e-commerce

metric_name: Network Security Assessment as Enterprise Requirement - Outcome
metric_value: Vulnerability management and security assessment became standard enterprise security practice; SIEM, vulnerability scanners, penetration testing became multi-billion dollar markets
notes: Aberdeen's prediction proved accurate; modern tools like Qualys, Tenable, Rapid7 built on ISS pioneering approach

metric_name: SAFEsuite vs NetProbe vs PingWare Comparison
metric_value: SAFEsuite comparable to competitors in ease of use/install/configure; outshines with more comprehensive vulnerability coverage, prioritized risk analysis, and update frequency
notes: Based on Aberdeen user interviews comparing all three commercial alternatives

metric_value: Real-time intrusion monitoring; monitors packet flow; recognizes attack patterns; responds from email notification to connection termination
notes: Earliest commercial IDS/IPS product documented by Aberdeen; precursor to modern SIEM

metric_value: Internet suppliers release products with 3-month cycles; quality suffers; security closely related to quality; Web-enabled applications are inherently vulnerable
source_page: Ensuring That Electronic Commerce Web Servers Are Secure
notes: Early documentation of speed-to-market vs security trade-off; remains central tension in modern software development

metric_value: Customers include AMP, Chevron, Department of Energy, First Union, Intel, IBM, JC Penney, Lockheed-Martin, MCI, Merck, Motorola, NASA, the Pentagon, Texas Instruments
source_page: Known By the Company One Keeps
notes: Blue-chip enterprise and government customer list validates market acceptance

metric_value: Privately held, venture-capital backed; does not release financial results at time of study
notes: Founded 1994 by Christopher Klaus; Tom Noonan President; privately held in 1996

metric_name: TCP/IP as Universal Security Attack Surface
metric_value: TCP/IP has become the common alphabet of the modern information age; backbone for Internet, Intranet, remote access; also universal attack vector
notes: Aberdeen identifies the fundamental duality of TCP/IP as both enabler and vulnerability surface

metric_value: Spending IS budget on security controls without understanding vulnerabilities or continuously monitoring is blindly throwing money away
notes: Risk-based security spending argument that became foundational to modern security frameworks
